channel.title: math.SP updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.SP updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: A note on the distribution of Neumann eigenvalues of the Laplacian on a Euclidean convex domain
item.description: arXiv:2603.16135v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We establish two universal inequalities for Neumann eigenvalues of the Laplacian on a Euclidean convex domain.
item.dc:creator: Kei Funano

item.title: Second Variation Formula for the Laplace Eigenvalue Functional on Closed Manifolds
item.description: arXiv:2603.16324v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: For a closed Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$ of dimension $n$, let $\lambda_{1}(g)$ be the first positive eigenvalue of the Laplace--Beltrami operator $\Delta_{g}$ and $\mbox{Vol}(M,g)$ the volume of $(M, g)$. Considering the scale-invariant quantity $\lambda_{k}(g)\mbox{Vol}(M,g)^{2/n}$ as a functional over all the metrics in a fixed conformal class, we derive a second variation formula for the functional. As a corollary, we prove that if the canonical flat metric on a torus is such that the multiplicity of $\lambda_{1}$ is two, then the flat metric is not a maximal point of the functional in its conformal class. This is a higher dimensional extension of Karpukhin's very recent work.
item.dc:creator: Kazumasa Narita

item.title: Ergodicity in discrete-time quantum walks
item.description: arXiv:2603.16837v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We undertake a detailed analysis of ergodicity for homogeneous discrete-time quantum walks on the integer lattice. The most significant result of our paper holds in dimension one, and gives a complete equivalence between the absolutely continuous spectrum of the unitary operator encoding the walk, and the equidistribution of its dynamics in position space, which appears for the first time in the context of large-volume quantum ergodicity. In higher dimensions, we give a criterion for full and partial ergodicity in terms of a finer property of the spectrum which we dub ``No Repeating Graphs'', and distinguish how strongly the equidistribution is taking place (weak convergence vs total variation). The paper includes a wealth of examples where we apply our criteria, with certain families of walks fully characterized.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Kumar, Mostafa Sabri

item.title: Two new universal inequalities for Neumann eigenvalues of the Laplacian on a planar convex domain
item.description: arXiv:2511.18043v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We establish two new universal inequalities for Neumann eigenvalues of the Laplacian on a planar convex domain.
item.dc:creator: Kei Funano
